arguments.task: Open the official WhatsApp developer documentation and gather evidence for: API authentication methods, whether access is self-serve or requires approval/paid plan, API surface type (REST or GraphQL), rate limit notes, and any official links related to pricing, onboarding, Cloud API, management API, and throughput limits. Also identify whether the docs mention a model context protocol or MCP. Return the relevant URLs and quoted snippets.
Open the official WhatsApp Cloud API docs and gather evidence for API type, authentication, onboarding/access requirements, and throughput/rate-limit notes. Return the URLs and quoted snippets.

Open the official WhatsApp getting started/onboarding docs and gather evidence for whether developers can self-serve access, what accounts/apps/tokens are needed, and whether Meta app review or business verification is required. Return the URLs and quoted snippets.

Open the official WhatsApp Business Management API docs and gather evidence for the existence and breadth of management endpoints, authentication method, and API protocol. Return the URLs and quoted snippets.
Open the official WhatsApp pricing docs and gather evidence for pricing/access model and any free tier or trial claims. Return the URLs and quoted snippets.
Check whether WhatsApp Business or Meta/WhatsApp has an official MCP server listing on modelcontextprotocol.io or clearly relevant official MCP listing pages. Return the URL and finding.

input.thought: Launch parallel browser tasks against official docs and MCP registry to collect verifiable evidence.
Poll all browser runs for completed evidence extraction.

arguments.task: Open the official WhatsApp Cloud API get started page. Extract direct quotes and URLs showing prerequisite accounts, self-serve onboarding steps, creation of a system user and permanent access token, and any note indicating when business verification or review affects higher throughput or production use.

Open the official WhatsApp access tokens page. Extract direct quotes and URLs showing the token types used for authentication, whether they are OAuth/OAuth2 or another token model, and any header format examples like Bearer tokens.
Open the official WhatsApp throughput page. Extract direct quotes and URLs showing message throughput limits and any request rate limits or per-user pacing limits.
Open the official WhatsApp Business Management API page and extract direct quotes and URLs showing the categories/breadth of management endpoints and whether it uses the same HTTP Graph API style.

Inspect the official Model Context Protocol servers repository src directory and determine whether any official WhatsApp or Meta server exists. Return the page URL and a concise finding based only on what is visible.

input.thought: Run targeted follow-up extractions to resolve remaining ambiguities around auth classification, onboarding, breadth, and MCP existence.

raw_result.description: Business messaging platform for sending and managing WhatsApp conversations, templates, phone numbers, and analytics via Meta-hosted APIs.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs describe Bearer access tokens and say authentication uses OAuth access tokens, but also note 'OAuth (not 2.0)' and discuss system user, business integration system user, and user access tokens; auth classification is therefore somewhat ambiguous.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official get-started docs describe self-serve onboarding through creating a Meta app, business portfolio/WABA setup, and generating permanent access tokens; pricing is usage-based rather than contact-sales gated. Business verification affects improved functionality such as higher throughput.

api_surface.breadth: Cloud API for messaging plus Business Management API for programmatic management of WhatsApp Business accounts and associated assets including phone numbers, templates, and analytics; docs describe HTTP requests on Graph API with standard methods and JSON payloads.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official WhatsApp or Meta MCP server was found in the official modelcontextprotocol/servers repository src directory from the gathered evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Docs indicate multiple limits: up to 80 messages/second by default with upgrade paths, app can make 5000 requests/hour per app per active WABA, and a pair rate limit of about 1 message every 6 seconds to the same user; throughput docs also mention error codes such as 130429 and 131057.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs state the platform is built on Graph API and uses HTTP protocol; auth uses Bearer access tokens; onboarding includes creating a Meta app and a system user with permanent access token; pricing is per delivered template message with several free windows/exemptions; business verification can improve functionality such as higher throughput.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Auth method labeling is ambiguous because docs reportedly say 'OAuth (not 2.0) access tokens' while also presenting token-based Bearer auth; MCP nonexistence is based on negative evidence from the official MCP servers repository listing rather than an explicit statement from Meta.

result.description: Business messaging platform for sending and managing WhatsApp conversations, templates, phone numbers, and analytics via Meta-hosted APIs.
result.auth_notes: Official docs describe Bearer access tokens and say authentication uses OAuth access tokens, but also note 'OAuth (not 2.0)' and discuss system user, business integration system user, and user access tokens; auth classification is therefore somewhat ambiguous.
result.gating_reason: Official get-started docs describe self-serve onboarding through creating a Meta app, business portfolio/WABA setup, and generating permanent access tokens; pricing is usage-based rather than contact-sales gated. Business verification affects improved functionality such as higher throughput.

api_surface.breadth: Cloud API for messaging plus Business Management API for programmatic management of WhatsApp Business accounts and associated assets including phone numbers, templates, and analytics; docs describe HTTP requests on Graph API with standard methods and JSON payloads.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official WhatsApp or Meta MCP server was found in the official modelcontextprotocol/servers repository src directory from the gathered evidence.

result.rate_limit_notes: Docs indicate multiple limits: up to 80 messages/second by default with upgrade paths, app can make 5000 requests/hour per app per active WABA, and a pair rate limit of about 1 message every 6 seconds to the same user; throughput docs also mention error codes such as 130429 and 131057.
result.notable_signals: Official docs state the platform is built on Graph API and uses HTTP protocol; auth uses Bearer access tokens; onboarding includes creating a Meta app and a system user with permanent access token; pricing is per delivered template message with several free windows/exemptions; business verification can improve functionality such as higher throughput.
result.human_review_reason: Auth method labeling is ambiguous because docs reportedly say 'OAuth (not 2.0) access tokens' while also presenting token-based Bearer auth; MCP nonexistence is based on negative evidence from the official MCP servers repository listing rather than an explicit statement from Meta.
